g.data-narration: Welcome to Programming with Google App Engine. This lecture covers PaaS architecture, core services, and deployment.
g.text: Module 5: Cloud Programming and Software Environments

g.data-narration: Google App Engine operates at the Platform-as-a-Service level, between IaaS infrastructure and SaaS software.
GAE is a PaaS that abstracts infrastructure management, handling scaling, load balancing, and health checking automatically.
g.text: Focus ONLY on writing application code

g.data-narration: GAE offers two runtime environments: Standard with sandboxed execution and free tier, and Flexible using Docker containers.
g.text: Python, Java, Node.js, Go, PHP, Ruby
Docker containers • Custom runtimes • SSH access

g.data-narration: GAE provides built-in services including Datastore for NoSQL, Cloud Storage for files, Memcache for caching, and Task Queues.
The GAE development workflow follows six steps: Design, Develop locally, Test, Deploy, Monitor, and Scale.
GAE supports web frameworks like Flask and Django. Here's a Flask example showing routes and Datastore integration.
Datastore is a NoSQL database using entities with kinds and properties. Supports queries, ancestor queries, and transactions.
GAE offers three scaling types: Automatic based on request rate, Manual with fixed instances, and Basic for intermittent workloads.
GAE integrates with Google Cloud IAM and provides the Users API for authentication. Access control is configured in app.yaml.
Deploy using 'gcloud app deploy'. Supports version management with traffic splitting between multiple deployed versions.
Key takeaways: GAE is a PaaS that handles infrastructure automatically, offers Standard and Flexible environments, and provides integrated services for building scalable applications.
